name: Mexican Zucchini Cheese Soup
instructions:
1. Heat the Olive Oil in a large pot over medium-high heat until shimmering. Dice the Onion and mince the Garlic, then add to the pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is tender and fragrant, about 5 minutes. Season with the Dried Oregano.

2. Pour in the Chicken Broth and add the Canned Tomatoes with their juices, breaking up any large pieces. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over high heat.

3. While waiting for the boil, dice the Zucchini and Yellow Squash into bite-sized pieces. Once boiling, stir the diced squash into the pot along with the Corn kernels and chopped Green Chile Peppers. Reduce the heat to low and simmer gently until the squash is tender but not mushy, about 10 minutes.

4. Cube the Processed Cheese into small pieces and stir into the soup. Continue to cook, stirring frequently, until the cheese is completely melted and the soup is creamy. Season with Black Pepper to taste.

5. Just before serving, roughly chop the Fresh Cilantro and stir it into the soup for a burst of fresh flavor. Serve hot with tortilla chips or crusty bread.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook the diced Onion and minced Garlic in Olive Oil until tender, then season with Dried Oregano.

2. Add the Chicken Broth and Canned Tomatoes, then bring to a boil.

3. Add the diced Zucchini, Yellow Squash, Corn, and Green Chile Peppers, then simmer for 10 minutes.

4. Stir in the cubed Processed Cheese until melted and creamy, then season with Black Pepper.

5. Add the chopped Fresh Cilantro just before serving hot.
